name: Insurance Renewal Risk Analysis
description: Fan-in analytics agent: consumes a policy_details payload (insurance.policy_record) AND a claim_status payload (insurance.claim_status) and computes loss ratio, and combined ratio when an expense figure is available, with a breach flag against a firm-configured renewal target. Computes from the supplied policy + claim data; does not fetch data and does not take a policy_id directly.

skills.name: analyze_renewal_risk
skills.description: Analyse renewal risk for a commercial policy from its policy and claims data: loss ratio, combined ratio when an expense figure is available, and a firm-policy breach flag versus a configured renewal target — never a universal loss-ratio cutoff.
examples: should we reprice this policy at renewal given the open claims
what is the loss ratio on this policy
is this policy a renewal risk
what is the combined ratio for this policy
how bad are the claims on this policy relative to premium
flag any policies at risk of non-renewal
is the loss ratio above our renewal target for this policy

consumes.select: {'policy_id': has(input.policy_id) ? input.policy_id : null, 'policy_name': has(input.policy_name) ? input.policy_name : null, 'insured_name': has(input.insured_name) ? input.insured_name : null, 'line_of_business': has(input.line_of_business) ? input.line_of_business : null, 'premium': has(input.premium) ? input.premium : null, 'premium_currency': has(input.premium_currency) ? input.premium_currency : null, 'coverage_limit': has(input.coverage_limit) ? input.coverage_limit : null, 'deductible': has(input.deductible) ? input.deductible : null, 'status': has(input.status) ? input.status : null, 'effective_date': has(input.effective_date) ? input.effective_date : null, 'expiry_date': has(input.expiry_date) ? input.expiry_date : null, 'as_of_date': has(input.as_of_date) ? input.as_of_date : null}

{'policy_id': has(input.policy_id) ? input.policy_id : null, 'claim_count': has(input.claim_count) ? input.claim_count : null, 'claims': has(input.claims) ? input.claims : null, 'claim_id': has(input.claim_id) ? input.claim_id : null, 'amount': has(input.amount) ? input.amount : null, 'status': has(input.status) ? input.status : null}
